episodes.title: Life, Joy, and Pleasure
episodes.author: Matt Tootle
episodes.summary: We turned to Psalm 16, where David prays, preserve me, O God, for in You I take refuge. We saw single-minded devotion: the Lord as chosen portion, no good apart from Him, and a glad heart that is not shaken. In Your presence is fullness of joy; at Your right hand are pleasures forevermore, secured through Christ who shows no corruption.

episodes.title: Who Can Dwell with God?
episodes.author: Brandon Nelson
episodes.summary: We turned to Psalm 15 and asked, who can dwell with God? The answer is one who walks blamelessly, does righteousness, speaks truth from the heart, and keeps promises even at personal cost. Such a person is beyond us. We are made fit to dwell with God only through Christ, the blameless one who cleanses us and counts His righteousness as ours by faith.

episodes.title: The Twelve Spies
episodes.author: Brandon Nelson
episodes.summary: We turned to Numbers 13-14, where twelve spies enter Canaan and ten bring a faithless report while Caleb trusts the Lord. Like them, our fear compares challenges to ourselves instead of to God. Because Christ, the true Joshua, has overcome sin and death and secures our promised rest, we are called to ongoing faith, not faithless wandering.
episodes.description:
Scripture Reading: Numbers 13:17-31; 14:19-25 (read by Tom)

The Twelve Spies

Brandon Nelson continues our series through Numbers with a look at the moment Israel stood on the edge of the Promised Land.

episodes.title: Life Together
episodes.author: Brandon Nelson
episodes.summary: We turned to 2 Corinthians 13:11-14, Paul's farewell. We saw the God who has brought us together, the triune God of love and peace whose life flows through our life as the church. Because the Son, the Spirit, and the Father live in us, we were called to agree, greet one another, and be made complete — then go in peace, grace, and love.

episodes.title: From Glory To Glory
episodes.author: Brandon Nelson
episodes.summary: We turned to Exodus 34 and 2 Corinthians 3, where Moses's face shone after meeting with God. We saw God's glory seen and heard as His word is proclaimed among His people, yet surpassed in Christ and the new covenant. With unveiled faces, we behold His glory and are transformed into His image from one degree of glory to another by the Spirit.

episodes.title: Show Me Your Glory
episodes.author: Brandon Nelson
episodes.summary: We turned to Exodus 33-34, where Moses prays, show me your glory. We saw the request reveals a heart that desires God above all else. God responds by reframing His glory as goodness and proclaiming His name, a God merciful and gracious, abounding in steadfast love. And we see this glory most fully revealed in Jesus, the radiance of God's glory, in whom mercy and justice meet at the cross.

episodes.title: The Golden Calf
episodes.summary: We turned to Exodus 32, where Israel makes a golden calf while Moses is on the mountain. We saw idolatry as trusting created things for hope, happiness, significance, and security instead of the Lord who goes before us. Because God is both merciful and just, Moses's intercession points us to Jesus, the greater mediator whose atoning sacrifice satisfies mercy and justice for all who trust Him.

episodes.title: Let Us Draw Near
episodes.summary: We turned to Exodus 24:1-11, asking how sinful people can draw near to a holy God. We saw Christ is a better mediator because He is the mediator of a better covenant, the new covenant established by His own blood once for all. And He enables true communion, inviting all who trust Him to behold His glory, eat at His table, and welcome one another in love.

episodes.title: Mount Sinai: Call and Commission
episodes.summary: We turned to Exodus 19, where God calls His people at Mount Sinai to Himself, to His purpose, and to a life of obedience. Because Christ perfectly fulfilled this calling on our behalf, establishing a new covenant by His blood, we now draw near to God: treasured as His possession, set apart as a holy nation, and sent as kingdom priests to declare His praises.

episodes.title: Risen Indeed
episodes.summary: We turned to Luke 24:13-35 and followed the road to Emmaus, where Jesus found discouraged disciples, showed them that all the Scriptures point to Him, and made Himself known in the breaking of the bread. Because the Christ had to suffer and enter His glory, our dashed hopes can give way to living hope, burning hearts, and glad witness that He is risen indeed.

episodes.title: Striving Together
episodes.summary: We turned to Exodus 17:8-18:27 and saw three lessons for striving together: the source of victory, the call to grow and go together for the sake of the gospel, and the wisdom of shared ministry. Because the Lord is our banner and Jesus never grows weary, we can bear one another's burdens, welcome outsiders with good news, and share the work so the body is built up in peace.

episodes.title: Water from the Rock
episodes.summary: We turned to Exodus 17:1-7 through the names Meribah and Massah and saw our quarreling, our testing of the Lord, and then His mercy at the rock. Because Christ is the true Rock who was struck for us, giving the water of life, we are called to stop judging God by our hardship and instead drink, remember, pray, and praise.

episodes.title: Wilderness Training
episodes.summary: We turned to Exodus 15:22-16:36 and saw that the wilderness is God's training ground, where hardship exposes what is in our hearts and teaches us daily dependence on His Word. Because He shows grace to grumblers and gives us Jesus, the true bread of life, we can trust His goodness in the in-between places, receive His provision with gratitude, and remember His faithfulness.

episodes.title: I Am and I Will
episodes.summary: We turned to Exodus 15:1-21 and heard a song of fulfillment shaped by God's promise: I am, I will, and you will know. We learned that Yahweh is victorious, supreme, the Redeemer, and forever. Because this song finds its fullest meaning in Christ's triumph over sin and death, we are called to worship Him alone and live as a redeemed people who know their God.

episodes.title: The Red Sea
episodes.summary: We turned to Exodus 13:17-14:31 and saw the Lord lead His people by a way they would not have chosen, hemming them in so His saving power would be unmistakable at the Red Sea. Because He alone fights for His people and makes a way through judgment, we are called to trust Him when the path feels impossible and follow Him in faith through the deliverance He provides.

episodes.title: The Passover
episodes.summary: We turned to Exodus 11:1-13:16 and saw the Lord distinguish His people through the Passover lamb, redemption by blood, and the call to remember His mighty deliverance. Because Christ is our true Passover Lamb whose sacrifice brings us out from judgment and slavery, we are called to shelter under His blood, walk in grateful obedience, and tell the coming generations what the Lord has done.

episodes.title: The Plagues
episodes.summary: We turned to Exodus 7-12 and saw the Lord confront Egypt's false gods, harden and humble Pharaoh, and make His name known through judgment and mercy. Because the gospel shows God's justice and rescue meeting fully in Christ, we are called to fear the Lord, refuse hard-hearted unbelief, and trust the One who alone can deliver us from bondage.

episodes.title: Delay, Disillusionment, and the Faithfulness of God
episodes.summary: We turned to Exodus 4:18-6:13 and faced the bitter moment when obedience seemed to make everything worse, and Israel's hopes gave way to delay and disillusionment. Because the Lord remembers His covenant and acts in faithfulness even when we cannot see it, we are called to bring Him our confusion, resist despair, and wait for the redemption He will surely accomplish.

episodes.title: The Burning Bush
episodes.summary: We turned to Exodus 3:1-14, where the Lord met Moses in the burning bush and revealed Himself as I AM. Because God comes down to deliver His people and ultimately makes Himself known in Christ, we were called to trust His holy presence, receive His mercy, and obey His commission even when we feel hesitant or insufficient.

episodes.title: God Is at Work, Even Here.
episodes.summary: We studied Exodus 2:11-21 and saw that even in Moses's failure, exile, and obscurity, God was still at work. Because the Lord does not abandon His purposes and brings deliverance through a better rescuer in Christ, we were encouraged to trust Him in hidden places, repent of self-reliance, and look for His faithful hand even here.

episodes.title: Blessing and Opposition
episodes.summary: We looked at Exodus 1:1-2:10 and saw God blessing His people even as Pharaoh opposed them with fear, violence, and death. Because the Lord preserves His covenant purposes and advances them through the weak until they reach their fulfillment in Christ, we were urged to take heart, resist fear, and remain faithful when obedience meets pressure.

episodes.title: The Exodus of Jesus
episodes.summary: We turned to Mark 1:9-15 and saw Jesus step into the waters, face the wilderness, and announce that the kingdom of God is at hand. Because He stands in the place of His people and succeeds where Israel failed, we were called to repent, believe the gospel, and follow Him into the new exodus He brings.

episodes.title: Why Bethlehem?
episodes.summary: We studied Matthew 2:1-12 and asked why the Christ was born in Bethlehem. Because God keeps His promises, sends the true shepherd-king in Jesus, and welcomes worship from the nations, we were invited to come to Him with glad surrender, not hardened resistance, and to rejoice that His humble coming reveals His saving rule.

episodes.title: Joyful Surrender
episodes.summary: We looked at Matthew 13:10-17 and 44-46 and saw the kingdom of heaven as a treasure worth losing everything to gain. Because God opens blind eyes to the surpassing worth of Christ and gives us the kingdom by grace, we were called to joyful surrender, glad repentance, and wholehearted faith rather than clinging to lesser treasures.

episodes.title: Firm Foundation: What We Believe about the Church Part II
episodes.summary: We studied Acts 2:41-47 and saw the practices of the church taking shape in a common faith, a common life, and a common witness. Because the gospel is the door into this people through Christ's death and resurrection, we were called to devote ourselves to the apostles' teaching, fellowship, breaking of bread, prayer, glad generosity, and a life together that points others to Jesus.

episodes.title: Firm Foundation: What We Believe about the Church Part I
episodes.summary: We studied Acts 2:42 and the opening chapters of Acts to see that the church is a community, a family, a temple, and a kingdom outpost. Because Jesus reconciles us to God through the cross and pours out His Spirit upon His people, we were called to live together as His gathered people, not as isolated consumers of religious events.

episodes.title: Firm Foundation: What We Believe about the Holy Spirit
episodes.summary: We studied Acts 2:1-21 and saw the promise of the Spirit fulfilled in the empowering presence of the Spirit. Because the ascended Jesus sends His Spirit to give life, transform us, and bear witness to Christ, we were called to rely on His power, seek His fruit and gifts, and fix our attention on Jesus rather than the signs themselves.

episodes.title: Firm Foundation: What We Believe about Salvation
episodes.summary: We turned to John 3:16-21 to ask four questions about salvation: why we need to be saved, who saves us, how we can know we are saved, and what we are saved for. Because we were perishing under God's wrath, the Father gave His Son and the Spirit grants new birth, so we look to Christ in faith, come into the light, and live for the God who rescued us.

episodes.title: Firm Foundation: What We Believe about Jesus
episodes.summary: We turned to John 1:1-18, alongside Colossians 1, to see who Jesus is. We saw that His story begins at the beginning, that He is fully God and fully man, and that He is God's Son. Because He lived, died, and rose again for us, we are called not only to know the truth about Jesus, but to trust Him and stake our lives on Him.

episodes.title: Firm Foundation: What We Believe about Sin
episodes.summary: We turned to Romans 5:12-17 to face two questions: what sin is and what the solution is. We saw sin in our actions, our desires, and our very nature in Adam, leaving us under death and condemnation. Because Christ is the better Adam whose grace overflows to sinners, we receive His gift of righteousness and learn to hate sin while relying on His grace.

episodes.title: Firm Foundation: What We Believe about Humanity
episodes.author: Brandon Nelson
episodes.summary: We turned to Genesis 1:26-28, with the light of Christ in Colossians, to ask who we are and why we are here. We saw that we are creatures, image bearers, and embodied souls, made to reflect God's glory, rule His world, and reproduce His image. Because Jesus restores what sin has marred, we find our identity in Him and live for His glory in everyday life and mission.

episodes.title: Firm Foundation: What We Believe About Scripture
episodes.author: Brandon Nelson
episodes.summary: We turned to 2 Timothy 3:14-4:5 to see the nature and purpose of Scripture. We saw that God's breathed-out Word brings revelation, salvation, and transformation, making us wise for salvation through faith in Christ Jesus and equipping us for every good work. Because Scripture leads us to Christ and trains us in His ways, we receive it and keep the Word central in the church.

episodes.title: Firm Foundation: What We Believe About God
episodes.summary: We turned to Acts 17:16-34 to hear of the God who is creator, sustainer, ruler, near, Father, judge, and Savior. Because this God has made Himself known in Jesus, who died and rose again, we are not left to invent Him in our own image. We are called to turn from idols, repent, believe, and bear witness to the living God.

episodes.title: I Will Build My Church
episodes.author: Brandon Nelson
episodes.summary: We turned to Matthew 16:13-28 and the promise, I will build my church. We saw that our confidence rests in the person of Jesus, the Christ and Son of the living God, and in His cross, where He conquered death and secured His people. Because the church is His work and His gift, we deny ourselves, take up our cross, and follow Him together.

episodes.title: Birth 2.0
episodes.author: Bernard Howard
episodes.summary: We turned to John 2:23-3:15, where Jesus tells Nicodemus that we must be born again. We saw the why of the new birth, the what of the new birth, and the how of the new birth: sinners cannot enter God's kingdom, but through Jesus' sacrificial death and the Spirit's work we are washed, made alive, and given power for a new life of love and obedience.

episodes.title: One Voice: Glorifying God through Our Witness
episodes.author: Brandon Nelson
episodes.summary: We turned to Romans 15:14-33 and closed the series on glorifying God with one voice. We saw the church's priestly mission: to bring the gospel to the nations, that the offering of the Gentiles may be acceptable, sanctified by the Holy Spirit. Because Christ does this work, we join Him in obedience and partnership through prayer and generosity.

episodes.title: One Voice: Glorifying God through Our Welcome
episodes.author: Brandon Nelson
episodes.summary: We turned to Romans 15:5-7 and saw that the church glorifies God with one voice by welcoming one another as Christ welcomed us. Because Jesus received us by mercy, we were called to repent of partiality, bear with one another in love, and make our life together a visible witness to the gospel.

episodes.title: One Voice: Glorifying God through Our Worship
episodes.author: Brandon Nelson
episodes.summary: We turned to Romans 15:1-13, opening a series on the church's purpose. We saw the glory of God, His weighty presence revealed in creation, in His word, and most brightly in the face of His Son. And we saw the worship of God: one voice offering spiritual sacrifices in Spirit and in truth, presenting our bodies as a living sacrifice, the fruit of lips that confess His name.

episodes.title: The Short-Sighted Fool
episodes.author: Brandon Nelson
episodes.summary: We turned to Psalm 14 and saw the fool's refusal to reckon with God, a corruption that exposes every human heart's need. Because salvation comes from Zion through Christ, who alone is righteous, we were warned against practical unbelief and invited to find refuge and glad hope in the Lord.

episodes.title: A Life of Deep Faith
episodes.author: Matt Tootle
episodes.summary: We turned to Psalm 13 and learned to bring our sorrow and unanswered questions honestly before the Lord. Because His steadfast love and salvation are sure in Christ, deep faith does not deny grief but carries it to God, asks Him to light our eyes, and learns to sing because He has dealt bountifully with us.

episodes.title: The Faithful and The Wicked
episodes.author: Caleb Cleveland
episodes.summary: We turned to Psalm 12 and heard the cry, Save, O Lord, as the faithful seem to vanish and deceit fills the world. Because the Lord's words are pure and His promises are trustworthy, we were called to depend on Him, resist flattering lips, and hope in the God who guards His people from this generation forever.

episodes.title: The Holiness of Hunkerin'
episodes.author: Brandon Nelson
episodes.summary: We turned to Psalm 11, where David says, in the Lord I take refuge. We saw three reasons for holy hunkering down: the Lord is in His holy temple, His throne in heaven, so He hasn't changed address; He uses troubles to test the righteous; the upright shall behold His face. So we stand firm, knowing our King will never leave and that the foundation of His throne stands forever.

episodes.title: An Attentive God in Troubling Times
episodes.author: Nic Horn
episodes.summary: We turned to Psalm 10 and voiced the anguish of troubling times, when God seems far and the wicked prosper. Because the Lord sees, hears, and will arise to defend the helpless, we were invited to pray honestly, trust His justice, and entrust the vulnerable to the King who sets all things right.

episodes.title: Defiant Praise
episodes.author: Brandon Nelson
episodes.summary: We turned to Psalm 9 and heard defiant praise rising in the face of enemies, injustice, and affliction. Because the Lord reigns forever, judges with righteousness, and does not forget the afflicted, we were called to recount His deeds, trust His name, and praise Him as the stronghold who lifts His people.

episodes.title: Strength Through Weakness
episodes.author: Tom Mundie
episodes.summary: We turned to Psalm 8 and saw the Lord's majesty displayed through the weakness of human creatures and the glory of creation. Because Jesus, the true Son of Man, was crowned with glory through suffering and reigns over all, we were humbled to trust God's power made visible through weakness.

episodes.title: The Righteousness of God
episodes.author: Brandon Nelson
episodes.summary: We turned to Psalm 7 and brought our longing for vindication before the righteous Judge. Because God tests hearts, opposes the wicked, and appointed judgment through the risen Christ, we were called to repent rather than presume, entrust vengeance to Him, and take refuge in the One whose righteousness saves.

episodes.title: Beauty Through Brokenness
episodes.author: Brandon Nelson
episodes.summary: We turned to Psalm 6 and saw that lament gives words to weakness, tears, and the ache of a broken story. Because the Lord hears our weeping and Christ entered our suffering to redeem it, we were invited to bring our pain to God, trust His mercy, and find beauty in His larger story.

episodes.title: Lead Me in Your Righteousness
episodes.author: Brandon Nelson
episodes.summary: We turned to Psalm 5 and prayed for the Lord to hear our groaning, oppose wickedness, and lead us in His righteousness. Because we enter His presence only through the abundance of His steadfast love and the righteousness given in Christ, we were called to seek Him morning by morning and rejoice under His favor.

episodes.title: Dwelling in Safety
episodes.author: Brandon Nelson
episodes.summary: We turned to Psalm 4 and saw a prayer that moves from distress to quiet confidence in the Lord. Because God has set apart the godly for Himself and answers His people through Christ, we were called to bring our anxiety to Him, turn from false hopes, receive His gladness, and lie down in safety.

episodes.title: Save Me O God
episodes.author: Brandon Nelson
episodes.summary: We turned to Psalm 3 and learned to cry out for salvation when enemies, fear, and shame press in. Because Jesus was struck down yet rose again as the reigning King, we can bring our need to God, rest beneath His shield, and trust with confidence that salvation belongs to the Lord.

episodes.title: Christ Our King
episodes.author: Luke Sellers
episodes.summary: We turned to Psalm 2 and saw the nations rage against the Lord and His Anointed while God sets His King on Zion. Because Jesus is the risen Son who rules with justice and mercy, we were called to take refuge in Him, resist rebellion, and live with hope as His kingdom advances and every enemy falls.

episodes.title: Blessed is the Man
episodes.author: Brandon Nelson
episodes.summary: We turned to Psalm 1 and saw the blessed life pictured in the righteous man who delights in the law of the Lord. Because Jesus is the truly blessed Man who stands where sinners fail, we were called to turn from wicked counsel, meditate on God's Word, and grow in Christ.

episodes.title: Shaped by the Shema
episodes.author: Brandon Nelson
episodes.summary: We turned to Deuteronomy 6:4-9 and heard the call to listen to the Lord, love Him with all we are, and teach His Word diligently. Because He first loved us in Christ and binds us to Himself by grace, we were called to attend to Him, receive His love, and let His truth shape our homes and witness.

episodes.title: I Am the Vine
episodes.author: Brandon Nelson
episodes.summary: We turned to John 15:1-11, where Jesus declares, I am the true vine, and My Father is the gardener. We saw three things: Jesus is the true vine, the true Israel whose life springs up for us; the Father is the gardener who prunes fruitful branches; and we are the branches, called to abide in His love, keep His commandments, and bear much fruit that our joy may be full.

episodes.title: I Am the Way, the Truth, and the Life
episodes.author: Brandon Nelson
episodes.summary: We turned to John 14:1-7, where Jesus declares, I am the way, and the truth, and the life. We saw three things: He is the way to the Father, preparing a place for us in His Father's house; He is the truth, because knowing Him is knowing the Father; and He is the life, the only way to the Father, now revealed through His death and resurrection.

episodes.title: I Am the Resurrection and the Life
episodes.author: Brandon Nelson
episodes.summary: We turned to John 11:1-44, where Jesus declares, I am the resurrection and the life. We saw four assurances of His love: He came despite the threat to His life, He spoke the truth to Martha grounding her faith, He shared our grief and wept with us, and He raised Lazarus to display the glory of God, calling us to believe that He is the Christ, the Son of God.

episodes.title: I Am the Good Shepherd
episodes.author: Brandon Nelson
episodes.summary: We turned to John 10:1-18, where Jesus declares, I am the good shepherd, and I am the door. We saw three things: Jesus is the door of the sheep, the only way into salvation and pasture; He is the good shepherd who lays down His life for the sheep, unlike the hired hand who flees; and He calls us to hear His voice and follow Him.

episodes.title: I Am the Light of the World
episodes.author: Brandon Nelson
episodes.summary: We turned to John 8:12 and 9:1-41, where Jesus declares, I am the light of the world. We saw two things: as the light, Jesus has come into our darkness to give sight, exposing our sin and revealing the glory of God; and as His disciples, we are called to follow Him and no longer walk in darkness but have the light of life, believing and worshiping Him as Lord.

episodes.title: Jesus Is
episodes.author: Brandon Nelson
episodes.summary: We began a new series on the I am statements in John's gospel, looking at John 8:31-59, where Jesus says, before Abraham was, I am. We saw this name claims He is the eternal God, who alone gives life and freedom. Because He is the I am, His signs point us to trust Him as the Christ, the Son of God, and receive life in His name.

episodes.title: The Advance of the Gospel
episodes.author: Brandon Nelson
episodes.summary: We turned to Ephesians 6:18-24, where Paul shows how the gospel advances through the church. We saw two ways: through prayer, praying at all times in the Spirit with perseverance for all the saints, that the mystery of the gospel may be proclaimed boldly; and through mission partnership, as Paul sends Tychicus to encourage our hearts and asks us to support laborers for the gospel.

episodes.title: Christ at Work
episodes.author: Brandon Nelson
episodes.summary: We turned to Ephesians 6:5-9, where Paul shows that Christ is Lord of our work. We saw two things: as employees, we work not for earthly masters but for Christ with sincerity of heart, knowing our reward comes from the Lord; and as employers, we are to treat those under us with dignity and equality, knowing we too have a Master in heaven with whom there is no partiality.

episodes.title: Christ at Home
episodes.author: Brandon Nelson
episodes.summary: We turned to Ephesians 6:1-4, where Paul shows what a gospel-shaped home looks like. We saw two things: children are called to obey and honor their parents in the Lord, knowing Christ, doing what is right, and looking forward to His blessing; and fathers are called not to provoke their children to anger but to bring them up in the discipline and instruction of the Lord, dependent on His grace.

episodes.title: A Profound Mystery
episodes.author: Matt Tootle
episodes.summary: We turned to Ephesians 5:22-33, where Paul unveils the profound mystery of marriage. We saw that wives are called to submit to their husbands as to the Lord, and husbands are called to love their wives as Christ loved the church, giving Himself up to sanctify and present her in splendor. For this mystery is profound: it refers to Christ and the church, the two becoming one flesh.

episodes.title: Walk in the Light
episodes.author: Brandon Nelson
episodes.summary: We turned to Ephesians 5:1-14, where Paul calls us to walk as children of light. We saw three reasons: we walk in the light because we are no longer darkness but light in the Lord, our light must bear the fruit of all that is pleasing to Him, and we are to awaken from sleep because Christ will shine on us, exposing the hidden works of darkness.

episodes.title: Walk in Love
episodes.author: Brandon Nelson
episodes.summary: We turned to Ephesians 4:25-5:2, where Paul calls us to walk in love as Christ loved us. We saw four marks of the new life: putting away falsehood and speaking truthfully as members of one another; dealing with anger without sin and forgiving as God in Christ forgave us; replacing theft with honest work; and putting away corrupting talk for words that build up, as we seek to imitate God.

episodes.title: The New Self
episodes.author: Brandon Nelson
episodes.summary: We turned to Ephesians 4:17-24, where Paul contrasts the old self with the new self we have in Christ. We saw three things: the new self is different from the old self corrupted by deceitful desires, we are called to put off that old self, and we are called to put on the new self, created after the likeness of God in true righteousness and holiness, found only in Jesus.

episodes.title: Life Together
episodes.author: Brandon Nelson
episodes.summary: We turned to 2 Corinthians 13:11-14, Paul's farewell. We saw the God who has brought us together, the triune God of love and peace whose life flows through our life as the church. Because the Son, the Spirit, and the Father live in us, we were called to agree, greet one another, and be made complete — then go in peace, grace, and love.

episodes.title: An Unimpressive Tree
episodes.author: Brandon Nelson
episodes.summary: We turned to Isaiah 52:13-53:5, where the prophet's report reveals God's saving arm through the Lord's servant. We saw three things: God will reveal His power, this servant will appear unimpressive like a young plant and a root in dry ground, yet through His death He will bear much fruit. So we are called to own our own unimpressiveness, trust the Lord, and become fruitful disciples.

episodes.title: The Wondrous Mystery
episodes.author: Brandon Nelson
episodes.summary: We turned to Ephesians 3:1-13, where Paul unfolds the wonder of the gospel mystery. Because God has revealed what was once hidden, we see three things: the mystery that Gentiles are fellow heirs and members of the same body, the ministry given to Paul to make this mystery known, and the manifold wisdom of God now displayed through the church to rulers and authorities in heavenly places.

episodes.title: A Living Temple
episodes.author: Luke Sellers
episodes.summary: We turned to Ephesians 2:19-22, where Paul describes what we now are as those united to Christ. Because Christ is our cornerstone, we are no longer strangers but fellow citizens with the saints, members of God's household, and living stones being built together into a holy temple, a dwelling place for God by the Spirit.

episodes.title: A New Humanity
episodes.author: Brandon Nelson
episodes.summary: We opened Ephesians 2:11-22, where Paul shows that Christ has broken down the dividing wall between Jew and Gentile. Because Christ is Himself our peace, we who were once far off have been brought near by His blood; through His death He has made us one new humanity and given us access to the Father by the Spirit.

episodes.title: Saved by Grace
episodes.author: Brandon Nelson
episodes.summary: We turned to Ephesians 2:6-10, where Paul repeats, by grace you have been saved. We saw we are saved because we were dead in trespasses and in need of rescue. Yet by grace, God's unmerited favor in Christ, we have been saved through faith, the gift of God, not from ourselves. We are His workmanship, created in Christ Jesus for good works which God prepared for us to walk in.

episodes.title: Understanding Your Spiritual Condition
episodes.author: Richard Trucks
episodes.summary: We walked through Ephesians 2:1-10, where Paul lays out our spiritual condition apart from Christ and the rescue God has provided. Because God is rich in mercy, even when we were dead in trespasses and sins He made us alive together with Christ, raising and seating us with Him, that we might walk in the good works He prepared for us.

episodes.title: Knowing God
episodes.author: Brandon Nelson
episodes.summary: We turned to Ephesians 1:15-23, where Paul prays that we would know God more. Because the Father has raised Christ from the dead and seated Him at His right hand, we pursue three things: a deeper knowledge of the hope to which He has called us, the riches of His glorious inheritance in the saints, and the immeasurable greatness of His power toward us who believe.

episodes.title: Blessed in Christ (Pt. 2)
episodes.author: Brandon Nelson
episodes.summary: We turned to Ephesians 1:3-14 to see our redemption in light of the Trinity. Because the Father, Son, and Holy Spirit work together for our salvation, we trace three movements: our redemption was planned by the Father from before the foundation of the world, accomplished by the Son who unites all things in Himself, and applied by the Spirit who seals us until the day of redemption.

episodes.title: Blessed in Christ (Pt. 1)
episodes.author: Brandon Nelson
episodes.summary: We walked through Ephesians 1:3-14, where Paul praises God for the spiritual blessings that are ours in Christ. Because the Father has lavished His grace on us in the Beloved, we celebrate four blessings: our election before the foundation of the world, our adoption as sons, our redemption through Christ's blood, and our promised inheritance guaranteed by the Holy Spirit.

episodes.title: Who We Are
episodes.author: Brandon Nelson
episodes.summary: We opened Ephesians 1:1-2 to ask where our true identity is to be found. Because Christ has united us to Himself, our identity is no longer shaped by culture but by being in Christ: we are saints, set apart for Him, and faithful in Christ Jesus, called to live in light of who He has made us to be.

episodes.title: Why We're Here
episodes.author: Brandon Nelson
episodes.summary: We gathered around Matthew 28:16-20, where the risen Christ claims all authority in heaven and on earth and then sends us into the world to make disciples. Because Jesus has all authority, we walk in His authority by going, baptizing, and teaching all that He commanded, resting on His promise to be with us always.
episodes.description:
Scripture Passage: Matthew 28:16-20

1. Jesus Has All Authority
In verse 18, Jesus says, “All authority in heaven and on earth has been given to me.” Everything we do flows from this truth. Jesus is the King with authority over everything — lif
